title: The First Sunday in Lent
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## February 26, 2023 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

LITURGICAL CONTEXT
This is the First Sunday in Lent, the opening Sunday of the six-week penitential season preceding Easter. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would be Genesis 2:15-17, 3:1-7 (the temptation of Adam and Eve), Psalm 32, Romans 5:12-19 (Paul on sin and grace through Christ), and Matthew 4:1-11 (Jesus's temptation in the wilderness). The liturgical color is purple, and the season emphasizes repentance, fasting, and spiritual discipline leading toward Holy Week.

EPISCOPAL/ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS
The Episcopal Church was in the lead-up to General Convention (which would occur in June 2023), with ongoing debates about sexuality and doctrine. In the Diocese of Colorado, Bishop Kym Cole had been serving since 2018; the diocese was navigating post-pandemic recovery and the broader denominational tensions around LGBTQ+ inclusion and biblical interpretation that had intensified after the 2022 General Convention.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS
Denver was experiencing typical late-winter weather with recent snow and cold temperatures. The Colorado legislature was in session (began early February), with education funding and water rights dominating discussion. Locally, Denver's homeless crisis and encampment issues were ongoing concerns; Saint John's Cathedral sits in downtown Denver near areas affected by homelessness and poverty.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)
The Ohio train derailment in East Palestine (Feb 3) was still dominating headlines with ongoing environmental and health concerns. President Biden's classified documents controversy was developing (with FBI search of his Delaware home occurring Feb 10-11). The NFL Super Bowl LVII had just occurred (Feb 12, Kansas City Chiefs victory), and the college basketball season was in full swing. The gender-affirming healthcare debate was intensifying nationwide with legislative action in multiple states.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS
The war in Ukraine was approaching its first anniversary (Feb 24), with Russian offensives ongoing and significant loss of life and displacement continuing. Turkey and Syria were in the immediate aftermath of the devastating February 6 earthquakes that killed tens of thousands. China was easing COVID restrictions after protests, signaling major policy shift.

CULTURAL MOMENT
America was in a period of cultural exhaustion and polarization, with deep partisan divides over education, gender identity, religious expression, and government authority. The post-pandemic "return to normal" was unevenly experienced. A particular anxiety hung over religious institutions navigating generational differences on moral and social questions. In Denver specifically, there was a sense of civic concern about homelessness, addiction, and inequality alongside winter fatigue.
